ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Create a comic strip that tells one of this term's stories.
[ ] Explore [this timeline](https://www.heritage-history.com/index.php?c=resources&s=study-cat&h=ancient_rome&f=timeline) of Ancient Rome.
[ ] View [maps](https://www.heritage-history.com/index.php?c=resources&s=study-page&h=ancient_rome&f=historical_maps) of some Roman cities.

[ ] Make clay characters, and use them to act out one of this term's stories.
[ ] Learn about [Roman clothing](https://www.readingmuseum.org.uk/sites/default/files/downloads/Roman%20clothes%20-%20compressed_0.pdf), and make your own cut-out Roman man and woman. (Content warning: some figures are dressed in undergarments.)
[ ] The Romans used [milestones](https://www.anatolianroads.org/milestones/) much like we use highway mile markers. Sometimes milestones also included important political information or news. Create a milestone for a Roman road letting travelers know important information from one of this term's stories.

[ ] Create a skit that tells one of this term's stories. Perform it for friends or family.
[ ] Look for Roman references in magazines, business names, and architecture. For example: The Venus Beauty Salon, The Mercury Café.
[ ] Read about five ways [Roman technology ](https://www.slsc.org/roman-technology/)was ahead of its time.
